---
title: "2.3.1 The purpose of encryption"
subtitle: "Data Transmission P63"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 2 Directory / 返回 Unit 2 目录](../../index.html)

## Why Encryption Matters

### Protecting data on public networks

When data is transmitted over any public network (wired or wireless), there is always a risk of it being <span class="mark">intercepted</span> by, for example, a hacker. Under these circumstances, a hacker is often referred to as an <span class="term">eavesdropper</span> (窃听者).

<span class="term">Encryption</span> (加密) alters data into a form that is <span class="mark">unreadable by anybody for whom the data is not intended</span>:

::: {.tight-list}
- It <span class="mark">cannot prevent the data being intercepted</span>
- But it <span class="mark">stops it from making any sense</span> to the eavesdropper
- This is particularly important if the data is <span class="mark">sensitive or confidential</span> (e.g. credit card/bank details, medical history or legal documents)
:::

## Plaintext and Ciphertext

### How encryption transforms data

The original data being sent is known as <span class="term">plaintext</span> (明文). Once it has gone through an <span class="term">encryption algorithm</span> (加密算法), it produces <span class="term">ciphertext</span> (密文):

::: {.tight-list}
- **Plaintext** → original readable data
- **Encryption algorithm** → a complex piece of software that takes plaintext and generates an encrypted string
- **Encryption key** → secret parameter controlling the algorithm
- **Ciphertext** → encrypted data that is the result of putting plaintext through an encryption algorithm
- **Decryption algorithm** → reverses the process
- Only someone with the <span class="mark">correct decryption key</span> can decode (unscramble) the data
:::

## Types of Encryption

### Two main categories

::: {.tight-list}
**Symmetric encryption (对称加密):**
- <span class="mark">Same encryption key</span> used to encrypt and decrypt the message
- Fast but main drawback: <span class="mark">keeping the encryption key a secret</span> (key distribution problem)

**Asymmetric encryption (非对称加密):**
- Uses <span class="mark">two keys: public key and private key</span>
- Public key — made available to everybody
- Private key — only known to the computer user
- Both types of key are needed to encrypt and decrypt messages
:::

## Classroom Check

### Understand encryption purpose and need

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- <span class="mark">encryption cannot prevent interception but stops data making sense</span> to eavesdroppers
- <span class="mark">plaintext → encryption algorithm + key → ciphertext</span>
- only someone with the <span class="mark">correct decryption key</span> can decode the data
- symmetric: <span class="mark">same key</span> for encrypt and decrypt; main drawback is keeping the key secret
- asymmetric: <span class="mark">public key (available to all) + private key (kept secret)</span>; both needed
:::

## Bridge

### Next: Symmetric and Asymmetric Encryption

The next deck details how symmetric and asymmetric encryption methods differ and work.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 2 Directory / 返回 Unit 2 目录](../../index.html)
